overview: Azorius Initiative is a control-arcane deck that leverages the Initiative mechanic to cast spells from the top of your library, turning card draw into immediate board impact. It aims to stabilize early, out-value opponents through recursive spell-casting, and close out games with resilient threats or unexpected finishers.
win_conditions: Large threats cast from the top of your library, or combo pieces that win immediately.
Lorien Revealed itself can be a win condition if you can cast it multiple times or protect it while casting game-ending spells.

keep_criteria: Keep any hand with a land and a turn-1 or turn-2 play (e.g., Quantum Riddler, White Plume Adventurer, or a Lorien Revealed enabler).
Prioritize hands with 2-3 lands and at least one way to find or protect Lorien Revealed.
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan hands with 0-1 lands, multiple high-cost spells with no early plays, or hands lacking land acceleration/fixing.

early_game: Turn 1: Play a land, cast Quantum Riddler or White Plume Adventurer if possible.
Turn 2: Play a second land, cast Lorien Revealed or another Initiative creature.
If you have Lorien Revealed and an Initiative creature, you can start casting spells from the top of your library.
Sequence lands carefully to ensure you can hit your mana requirements for Lorien (4 mana) and your threats.
mid_game: Treat your library as a secondary hand.
Prioritize casting high-impact spells from the top when you have Initiative.
Manage mana to keep Lorien Revealed alive and maintain a board presence.
Don't overcommit to casting low-value spells from the top if you need mana for protection or interaction.
Commit threats when you have protection or a board wipe ready.
Hold back if the opponent has significant interaction or if you're behind on board.
Use your Initiative to answer threats or stabilize rather than just going wide.
late_game: Large threats cast from the top of your library, or combo pieces that win immediately.
Lorien Revealed itself can be a win condition if you can cast it multiple times or protect it while casting game-ending spells.
Go all-in when you have a clear board advantage, the opponent has no interaction, or you've established a reliable Initiative engine.
Also commit when you can cast a high-impact spell from the top that swings the board.

sequencing_tips: Initiative recursion is powerful but vulnerable to board wipes and targeted removal.
Be mindful of your library size
running out of cards can limit your Initiative value.
Practice sequencing your turns to maximize spell-casting from the top.
Common mistake: overcommitting to casting low-value spells from the top instead of saving mana for protection or interaction.

matchups.advice: Use multiple low-cost threats to force counters, then cast higher-value spells from the top. Utilize instant-speed interaction or protection spells. Don't overcommit to a single spell if you can split your threats.
Apply early pressure to disrupt their mana development. Use removal to kill their ramp creatures. If they get ahead, stabilize with board wipes and out-value them with Initiative recursion.
Prioritize early interaction and spot removal. Use your Initiative to cast removal or counterspells from the top. Don't overextend into board wipes; stabilize first, then close out.
Pressure with resilient threats that are hard to remove. Use your Initiative to out-value their card draw. Force them to use interaction on your threats, then swing with the top of your library.

Win the resource war. Prioritize protecting your Lorien Revealed and Initiative creatures. Use targeted removal to kill their key pieces. Sideboard in additional counterspells and graveyard hate if needed. Out-value them by casting more spells from the top.

common_threats: Watch for the opponent's graveyard (especially against Dredge/Oath), their mana base (to predict if they can hit 4+ mana for Lorien or key spells), and their interaction count.
Key tells include holding up blue mana (counterspell/spot removal) or black mana (Lurrus/Graveyard hate).
